SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.allocation

Class AllocationComparison

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.allocation.AllocationComparison
public class AllocationComparison extends Object
Compares the results of several AllocationMethods run on the same base case, auto-evaluates how much they disagree, reports how each method would benefit each owner (source), and recommends a method.

The recommendation follows the fidelity-versus-cost ladder of the three facility-split methods. Differences are measured as the maximum relative difference of any source's per-product (gas / oil / water) allocation between two methods:

  • If the all-in proxy and the stand-alone re-simulation disagree by more than the tolerance, commingling introduces material non-linear (compositional / thermal) coupling, so the most faithful method (AllocationMethod.STAND_ALONE) is recommended and a base-case refresh is flagged.
  • Else if the common recovery factor and the all-in proxy disagree by more than the tolerance, source-dependent routing matters but the coupling is weak, so the cheaper linear proxy (AllocationMethod.ALL_IN) is recommended.
  • Otherwise all methods agree within the tolerance, so the cheapest and most auditable common recovery factor (AllocationMethod.COMPONENT_RATIO) is recommended.
Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim Development Team

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static class 
    AllocationComparison.OwnerSensitivity
    One row of the owner-benefit table: a single source's allocation of a single product under each method, plus the spread across methods.

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    private static final ProductType[]
    COMPARED_PRODUCTS
    Product categories used for owner-benefit comparison.
    static final double
    DEFAULT_TOLERANCE
    Default agreement tolerance (relative, fraction) for the auto-recommendation.
    private static final double
    FLOW_NOISE_FLOOR
    Minimum absolute product flow (kg/hr) below which a relative difference is ignored as numerical noise.
    private String
    recommendationRationale
    The rationale string for the recommendation (computed lazily).
    private AllocationMethod
    recommendedMethod
    The recommended method (computed lazily).

    results
    The per-method allocation results, in run order.

    runtimeMillis
    The per-method wall-clock runtime in milliseconds.
    static final String

    Schema version for the JSON export.

    tolerance
    The agreement tolerance used for the recommendation.

    Description
    AllocationComparison(Map<AllocationMethod,ProductionAllocationResult> results, Map<AllocationMethod,Long> runtimeMillis)
    Creates a comparison from the given per-method results and runtimes.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    private void
    evaluate()
    Evaluates the pairwise agreement of the available methods and selects the recommended method and rationale.
    double
    getMaxRelativeDifference(AllocationMethod a, AllocationMethod b)
    Computes the maximum relative difference between two methods over every source's per-product (gas / oil / water) allocation.
    List<AllocationComparison.OwnerSensitivity>
    getOwnerSensitivity(String unit)
    Computes the owner (source) benefit table: for every source and product, the per-method allocation and the spread (maximum minus minimum across methods).
    String
    getRecommendationRationale()
    Gets the human-readable rationale for the recommendation, computing it on first use.
    AllocationMethod
    getRecommendedMethod()
    Gets the recommended method, computing it on first use from the pairwise agreement of the methods that were run.
    ProductionAllocationResult
    getResult(AllocationMethod method)
    Gets the result for a method.
    Map<AllocationMethod,ProductionAllocationResult>
    getResults()
    Gets all per-method results in run order.
    long
    getRuntimeMillis(AllocationMethod method)
    Gets the runtime of a method in milliseconds.
    AllocationComparison
    setTolerance(double tolerance)
    Sets the agreement tolerance (relative fraction) used by getRecommendedMethod().
    String
    toJson()
    Serialises the comparison (recommendation, pairwise differences, per-method residuals/runtimes, and owner sensitivity in kg/hr) to a pretty-printed JSON string.

  • Constructor Details

    • AllocationComparison

      public AllocationComparison(Map<AllocationMethod,ProductionAllocationResult> results, Map<AllocationMethod,Long> runtimeMillis)
      Creates a comparison from the given per-method results and runtimes.
      Parameters:
      results - the per-method allocation results, in run order; must be non-null and non-empty
      runtimeMillis - the per-method runtimes in milliseconds; must be non-null

  • Method Details

    • setTolerance

      public AllocationComparison setTolerance(double tolerance)
      Sets the agreement tolerance (relative fraction) used by getRecommendedMethod(). Default 0.01.
      Parameters:
      tolerance - the relative tolerance; must be positive
      Returns:
      this comparison for chaining

    • getResult

      public ProductionAllocationResult getResult(AllocationMethod method)
      Gets the result for a method.
      Parameters:
      method - the method; must have been run
      Returns:
      the result, or null if the method was not run

    • getResults

      public Map<AllocationMethod,ProductionAllocationResult> getResults()
      Gets all per-method results in run order.
      Returns:
      an unmodifiable view of the results map

    • getRuntimeMillis

      public long getRuntimeMillis(AllocationMethod method)
      Gets the runtime of a method in milliseconds.
      Parameters:
      method - the method; must have been run
      Returns:
      the runtime in milliseconds, or 0 if the method was not run

    • getMaxRelativeDifference

      public double getMaxRelativeDifference(AllocationMethod a, AllocationMethod b)
      Computes the maximum relative difference between two methods over every source's per-product (gas / oil / water) allocation. Products whose larger absolute flow is below the noise floor are ignored.
      Parameters:
      a - the first method; must have been run
      b - the second method; must have been run
      Returns:
      the maximum relative difference (fraction), or 0 if either method was not run

    • getRecommendedMethod

      public AllocationMethod getRecommendedMethod()
      Gets the recommended method, computing it on first use from the pairwise agreement of the methods that were run.
      Returns:
      the recommended method

    • getRecommendationRationale

      public String getRecommendationRationale()
      Gets the human-readable rationale for the recommendation, computing it on first use.
      Returns:
      the recommendation rationale

    • evaluate

      private void evaluate()
      Evaluates the pairwise agreement of the available methods and selects the recommended method and rationale.

    • getOwnerSensitivity

      public List<AllocationComparison.OwnerSensitivity> getOwnerSensitivity(String unit)
      Computes the owner (source) benefit table: for every source and product, the per-method allocation and the spread (maximum minus minimum across methods). A large spread for a source means the choice of method materially changes that owner's booked production.
      Parameters:
      unit - the flow unit for the table (for example kg/hr, kg/day, tonnes/year)
      Returns:
      owner-sensitivity rows, one per source-product combination with a non-trivial flow

    • toJson

      public String toJson()
      Serialises the comparison (recommendation, pairwise differences, per-method residuals/runtimes, and owner sensitivity in kg/hr) to a pretty-printed JSON string.
      Returns:
      the JSON representation
